name: Stran & Company, Inc.
business: Stran & Company is an outsourced marketing solutions provider that sells branded promotional products and related services. It purchases products from third-party manufacturers and resells them to customers, offering e-commerce, warehousing, fulfillment, and loyalty programs. It serves a diversified customer base across pharmaceutical, healthcare, manufacturing, gaming, technology, finance, construction, and consumer goods industries.

exposures.sentence: In addition to selling branded products, we offer our clients custom sourcing capabilities; a flexible and customizable e-commerce solution for promoting branded merchandise and other promotional products, managing promotional loyalty and incentives, print collateral, and event assets, order and inventory management, and designing and hosting online retail popup shops, fixed public retail online stores, and online business-to-business service offerings; creative and merchandising services; warehousing/fulfillment and distribution; print-on-demand; kitting; point of sale (POS) displays; and loyalty and incentive programs.
We offer a global solution for warehousing and fulfillment through a network of fulfillment providers including a 15-year relationship with industry leader Harte Hanks.
We serve a highly diversified customer base across many industry verticals including pharmaceutical and healthcare, manufacturing, gaming, technology, finance, construction and consumer goods.
We serve a highly diversified customer base across many industry verticals including pharmaceutical and healthcare, manufacturing, gaming, technology, finance, construction and consumer goods.
We have programs in place to detect, contain, and respond to data security incidents.
We serve a highly diversified customer base across many industry verticals including pharmaceutical and healthcare, manufacturing, gaming, technology, finance, construction and consumer goods.

We have also invested in an internal commercial Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system, Oracle/NetSuite's NetSuite ERP, which is expected to enhance the process of gathering and organizing the business data of our company through an integrated software suite, and was launched in the first half of 2025.

tamClaims.claim: The market for promotional products sales reached a record high of $27.7 billion in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: According to ASI, the market for promotional products sales reached a record high of $27.7 billion in 2025.

tamClaims.claim: The total addressable market is possibly up to $410 billion, based on the promotional products market ($27.7B), product packaging ($185B), loyalty incentives ($90B), printing ($86.6B), and trade show planning ($24.2B).
tamClaims.sentence: Moreover, the promotional products market is only one segment of a total addressable market of possibly up to $410 billion, based on the size of the promotional products market ($27.7 billion in 2025 according to ASI); the product packaging market ($185 billion as of 2021, according to Mordor Intelligence, a leading market intelligence and advisory firm); the loyalty incentive programs market ($90 billion annually according to the Incentive Marketing Association, the umbrella organization for suppliers in the incentive marketplace); the printing market ($86.6 billion projected for 2026, according to IBISWorld, an industry research provider); and the trade show and conference planning market ($24.2 billion projected for 2025, according to IBISWorld).
